text.title: Niche Evaluation (Researcher) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Niche Evaluation (Researcher) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each row of the report (sizing / competitor / accessibility / monetization / fit) MUST carry an evidence link. Unlinked rows are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unlinked rows drift into opinion; links keep the report defensible.

rule.statement: SAM/SOM cite ≥1 interview signal in addition to public sources. Sizing without behavioural anchor is flagged 'sizing-by-feel'.
rule.rationale: Public-report sizing alone misses the addressable shape; interview signal grounds the SAM.

rule.statement: Report ends with a single verdict (GO / PASS / PIVOT) and a one-sentence rationale citing the weakest scoring dimension.
rule.rationale: Reports without a verdict get re-litigated.

rule.statement: Competitor matrix lists ≥3 named competitors with price, segment, quality estimate, AND ≥1 evidence screenshot per row.
rule.rationale: Screenshots prevent verbal mis-quotes of competitor positioning.

rule.statement: Audience-accessibility scoring names ≥2 channels with cited CAC band (industry report or operator pilot).
rule.rationale: Unaccessible niches are unsellable; cited CAC anchors the score.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
